subject: The project README (or an equivalent top-level orientation document) for the myfi finance plugin, read cold by a newcomer deciding whether and how to install it.

dimensions.desc: Names the six-agent flock (advisor, quant, auditor, designer, worker, trader) and states the shape of the dispatch pipeline, not just that agents exist.
A newcomer can install the plugin and run a first command from the document alone: marketplace install, runtime requirements, and a concrete quickstart command.

Contains no leftover language from a generic engineering/sprint orchestration plugin: no 'engineering work', no @engineer, no @coder, no 'sprint', no generic pipeline/audit-swarm framing unrelated to finance.
Concrete and specific throughout: real command names (/myfi:analyze, /myfi:plan, /myfi:taxes, /myfi:trade), real toolkit/MCP surface, real config keys, not vague marketing prose.

guidance: Reward a document that a newcomer could act on immediately: install it, configure it, run a first command, and understand who the six agents are and why the toolkit/market-data contract exists. Penalize heavily any surviving engineering-sprint-orchestration language (an unrelated plugin's residue), vague platitudes with no concrete command/tool name, or a document that describes myfi's existence without letting the reader act.
